summary: We recommend shipping RESUME md at the repo root rather than under docs because a fresh Claude session or a new operator looking for the entry point will check the repo root first and the 5-file ordering puts QUEUE md and loop-prompt md first which are the two files that together encode the entire operating model. The tradeoff against a longer onboarding doc is that 5 files is a hard cap that forces the file to be navigational not exhaustive and that mirrors the project's overall ethos of small atomic artifacts. The file ends with a state-at-a-glance section that records the verified track status counts the live worker URL the bearer token path and the heartbeat cron job id plus an explicit next-action recommendation that says the orchestrator is currently in a no-action-needed state because B5 is the only meaningful pending and it is intentionally held for human merge.

claims.statement: RESUME md lists 5 files in priority order outcomes QUEUE md .claude loop-prompt md the operator-side memory file at the user home docs adrs 0001-rubric-calibration md and .claude agents README md because the file body declares exactly those 5 numbered headers in sequence with a one-paragraph rationale per file.

citations.title: RESUME md with the 5 numbered files
QUEUE md which is file 1 in the RESUME list

claims.caveats: File 3 in the list is the operator-side memory file at the user home directory which is not in the repo; the path is included so a reader knows where to look but the file is not committable.

claims.statement: the state-at-a-glance section records all 6 tracks with done blocked and in_progress counts because the file enumerates Track A 5 done Track B 4 done 1 in_progress Track C 5 done 2 blocked Track D 6 done Track E 4 done Track F 3 done plus this file finishing F4.

citations.title: QUEUE md which RESUME's counts derive from

claims.caveats: The counts are verified as of 2026-05-22T07 50 Z and will drift if a future tick advances B5 or unblocks C3 or C6; RESUME should be refreshed whenever a tick changes a track's done/blocked/in_progress distribution.

claims.statement: the file names what to do next when no operator guidance is given as a hold pattern because B5 is the only meaningful pending and is explicitly held for human merge so the orchestrator should keep firing every 5 minutes and continue logging the tick state without attempting a productive atomic step.

citations.title: outcomes-mcp pull 18 B5 floor ratchet PR held for human merge

claims.caveats: If the operator merges B5 or updates the C3 or C6 queue rows the hold-pattern recommendation should be refreshed to point at the new lowest-id pending.

open_questions: Should RESUME md be auto-generated from QUEUE md by a future tick so the state-at-a-glance section never drifts from the queue truth?
methodology_notes: Authored RESUME md in a single editing pass enumerating the 5 files with one-paragraph rationale each then appended the state-at-a-glance section verifying every count against QUEUE md and the live worker URL against the C2 runroc.
